details: Dependency-confusion package impersonating the @carvana namespace. On npm install, the preinstall hook runs index.js which collects system information (hostname, username, user ID, group ID, shell, current working directory, platform, architecture, home directory, OS type and release, total/free memory, CPU count) and the output of the whoami and id shell commands, then exfiltrates all data as a JSON POST request to hxxps://pleq9pugrugzr4zgyymazmnq0h68u4it[.]oastify[.]com/detox56 (an interactsh/oastify exfiltration endpoint). The package also ships a file containing harvested Instagram follower data from a prior victim. preinstall hook runs index.js which collects detailed system information (hostname, platform, architecture, home directory, username, uid, gid, shell, OS type/release, total/free memory, CPU count, current working directory, and the output of the Unix `whoami` and `id` commands) and POSTs the data as JSON to hxxps://pleq9pugrugzr4zgyymazmnq0h68u4it[.]oastify[.]com/detox56. The package name is a combosquat of the Carvana brand with an empty description and no repository. A separate bundled file contains an Instagram pending-follow-requests data dump.
